content.problemStatement:
Write a program that takes three space separated numbers as input and prints:


**"Increasing"** if the numbers are in strictly increasing order,
**"Decreasing"** if they are in strictly decreasing order,
and **"Neither"** otherwise.
Check the sample input / output below for further clarity.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers a, b and c.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one of the strings: "Increasing", "Decreasing" or "Neither" according to whether a<b<c, a>b>c, or neither condition holds.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 20 < 30 < 41, so the numbers are strictly increasing.
50 > 30 > 20, so the numbers are strictly decreasing.
The sequence is not strictly increasing nor strictly decreasing.

solution.approach: For each test case read the three integers. Compare them using strict inequalities. If a<b and b<c, the sequence is strictly increasing – output "Increasing". Else if a>b and b>c, it is strictly decreasing – output "Decreasing". Any other relation (including equal values or a mix of up and down) results in "Neither". The algorithm processes each test case independently in O(1) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.
